snp1.5:1.1:
Cunda the smith:
I ask the sage of abundant discernment,
snp1.5:1.3: awakened, lord of the Dhamma, free of craving,
snp1.5:1.4: supreme among two-legged beings, best of charioteers:
snp1.5:1.5:
How many contemplatives are there in the world?
Please tell me.
snp1.5:2.1:
The Buddha:
Four contemplatives, there is no fifth.
snp1.5:2.3: Being asked in person, I disclose them to you:
snp1.5:2.5: one who lives by the path, and the path-corrupter.
snp1.5:3.1:
Cunda:
Whom do the awakened call the path-victor?
snp1.5:3.3: How is one unequaled in pointing out the path?
snp1.5:3.4: When asked, tell me about the one who lives by the path.
snp1.5:3.5: Then disclose the path-corrupter to me.
snp1.5:4.1:
The Buddha:
Whoever, de-arrowed, has crossed over doubt,
snp1.5:4.2: is refreshed in unbinding, devoid of greed,
snp1.5:4.3: a guide to the world along with its devas:
snp1.5:4.4: The awakened call such a person path-victor.
snp1.5:5.1: Whoever here knowing the foremost as foremost,
snp1.5:5.2: who points out, who analyzes the Dhamma right here,
snp1.5:5.3: he, the cutter of doubt, the sage unperturbed,
snp1.5:5.4:
is called the second of monks:
path-explainer.
snp1.5:6.1: Whoever lives by the path, the Dhamma-way well-explained,
snp1.5:6.4:
is called the third of monks:
with the path as his life.
snp1.5:7.1: Making a façade of good practices,
snp1.5:7.2: brash, reckless, a corrupter of families,
snp1.5:7.4:
& going about as a counterfeit:
He is a path-corrupter.
snp1.5:8.2: —an instructed disciple of the noble ones, discerning—
snp1.5:8.3:
understands these, knowing,
“Not all are like that one,”
snp1.5:8.4: seeing one like that doesn’t kill his conviction.
snp1.5:8.6: the corrupt with the uncorrupt, the impure with the pure?
